structure.title: Substrate-bound E. coli ClpXP (ClpXΔN hexamer + substrate peptide)
structure.state: substrate-engaged spiral / lock-washer (5×ATPγS-loaded + 1×ADP seam); functional translocation state
structure.modeled_residues: ~62-414 per subunit (ClpXΔN; N-terminal Zn-binding domain res 1-61 deleted/unmodeled)

ring_geometry.symmetry_axis: substrate-peptide translocation axis (PCA of threaded substrate chain S)
ring_geometry.seam_subunit: F (ADP-bound) — the disengaged spiral seam, source of the native 5/6 coupler engagement
ring_geometry.special_interface: F->A / A->F seam: ADP subunit F breaks the sequential trans arginine-finger chain, giving native 5/6 engagement

functional_residues._numbering: UniProt P0A6H1 full-length; ClpXΔN construct = these minus offset (res62 = construct pos 1)
functional_residues.note: R307 is the TRANS arginine finger supplied by the adjacent subunit into its neighbor's ATP site — the direct ClpX analog of gp16's R146. E185 is the Walker-B catalytic glutamate (E185Q = classic dead-hydrolysis mutant).

N_term_res62.note: peripheral; N-terminus sits on the outer rim, ~47-49 Å off the translocation pore

C_term_res414.note: peripheral; native C-terminus is on the OUTER surface, ~62-65 Å off the pore — a direct C->N fusion does NOT approach the substrate channel (opposite of gp16, whose C-term res330 is a 6.2 Å DNA-channel contact)

direct_CtoN_forward.note: native C-term(i) -> next-subunit N-term(i+1) through-space gap around the ring; ~20 Å, short and bridgeable by a genetically encoded flexible tether, off-channel

direct_CtoN_reverse.note: wrong ring sense — 96 Å, not usable

circular_permutation.note: a CP would internally join native N(62)/C(414) — 54.7 Å apart — and open new termini at a surface loop; this is LONGER and gains nothing because the native termini are already off-channel

rationale: Native termini are peripheral: C-term res414 is 62-65 Å off the substrate pore, N-term res62 is 47-49 Å off it — a direct fusion never enters the translocation channel.
Direct C(i)->N(i+1) gap is only ~20 Å (19-23 Å), short enough for a genetically encoded flexible linker.
Circular permutation is strictly worse: it must bridge 54.7 Å internally and its only short-gap cut sites (~11-14 Å) sit 5-11 Å from the pore axis, i.e. inside the substrate channel.
Known-good single-chain ClpXΔN precedent: covalently tethered pseudohexamers (Martin/Baker/Sauer lineage) built by direct genetic linkage degrade substrate at near-WT rates.

design_principle: ClpX's native termini already sit on the outer rim, off the axial translocation channel, and the head-to-tail gap is only ~20 Å — so the optimal single-chain topology is a DIRECT flexible C->N fusion. Circular permutation (gp16's solution) is unnecessary and geometrically inferior here because ClpX's C-terminus, unlike gp16's DNA-channel-contacting res330, does not foul the functional pore.
